notes: Clean run with provider adapters forced to preview mode for synthetic submission artifacts.
Gemini 2.0 Flash Lite smoke run. Completed 1 task, reward 0.0. LiteLLM model-pricing warnings expected for unmapped OpenRouter model.
Configured OpenRouter model google/gemini-2.0-flash-001 reached provider but failed with upstream 429 rate limits and a final 504.

5 tasks, 1 trial. Pass@1=0.0. Root cause: Gemini Flash Lite does not call product-variant lookup tools before declining to process exchanges without item IDs. Write actions: 0/6 (0%). Read actions: 9/40 (22.5%). Model-class mismatch with published 42% reference (GPT-5). Admin-provided baseline (~42%) is the grading anchor; see baseline.md.

DeepSeek V3 agent (openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-chat) + Flash Lite user simulator, 30 tasks 1 trial. Fix 3 (proactive tool-use instruction in AGENT_INSTRUCTION) pushed write actions from 0% to 41.2% and pass@1 from 0.0 to 0.462 — matching the admin-provided ~42% published baseline. 4 infra errors (rate limit) excluded from metrics. Cost tracking shows $0 due to LiteLLM not having pricing data for these OpenRouter model aliases.

Gemini 2.0 Flash (non-lite), 5 tasks 1 trial, 1 infra error (upstream rate limit). Read actions improved to 51.7% vs. 22.5% for Flash Lite but write actions remain 0%. DB match: 0/4. Reward computation used Flash Lite as NL-assertions judge to avoid rate limits. Same root cause as Flash Lite: agent fails to complete multi-step tool chain for exchange/return write actions. Admin update confirmed: baseline is provided (~42%), one trial sufficient.
